comment: Merge duplicate allmoxy_customer_id rows that are really ONE customer into a single surviving record. Keyed by the AID being absorbed ('from'); 'into' is the surviving AID. apply_customer_merges.mjs folds the absorbed row's financials (lifetime/current totals, transactions, monthly_history, Stripe IDs, dates) into the survivor and removes the absorbed row. refresh_all applies it to allmoxy_core_customer (right after the core roster is built) and to customer_profiles (after enrichment), so the duplicate disappears from every downstream snapshot. Use when one real customer has two AIDs — e.g. re-onboarded under a new core id but same installer_id / HubSpot company / Stripe subscription.

2027._note: Mountain Showcase Group (#2027) is the same customer as #500 Mountain Showcase Group, Inc. — identical installer_id 1952, HubSpot company 16344570990, and Stripe subscription sub_1RcKTnHkM2QhTtxhyv1n5FOR. #2027 is an empty duplicate (never_paid, $0, 0 transactions).
